snp4.7:1.1:
Tissa-metteyya:
“Tell the damage, dear sir, for one given over to sexual intercourse.
snp4.7:1.5:
we’ll train in seclusion.”
The Buddha:
snp4.7:2.1: “In one given over to sexual intercourse,
snp4.7:3.2: but then resorts to sexual intercourse
snp4.7:3.4: is called vile in the world, a person run-of-the-mill.
snp4.7:4.3: Seeing this, he should train himself
snp4.7:5.2: he broods like a miserable wretch.
snp4.7:6.2: attacked by the words of others.
snp4.7:6.3: This, for him, is a great entanglement.
snp4.7:7.2: when he committed himself to the life alone,
snp4.7:7.3: but now that he’s given to sexual intercourse
snp4.7:8.3: stays firm in the life alone;
snp4.7:9.2: this, for the noble, is highest.
snp4.7:9.3: He wouldn’t, because of that, suppose himself to be better than others:
snp4.7:9.4:
He’s on the verge of unbinding.
People enmeshed in sensual pleasures, envy him:
snp4.7:10.1: a sage remote, leading his life
